title: lupinus pilosus subsp. luthereaui
intro: Lupinus pilosus subsp. luthereaui is a subspecies of the Lupinus pilosus plant, commonly known as the hairy lupine, and is a perennial flowering plant native to the southwestern United States. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Lupinus pilosus subsp. luthereaui include its ability to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed orally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
